name: Chad

colors.symbolism: the sky, hope, and the water resources of the south of the country
the sun and the deserts of the north
progress, unity and the sacrifice and blood shed for independence

description: Three equal vertical stripes — blue at the hoist, yellow in the centre, red at the fly. The design is identical to the flag of Romania apart from the precise shade of blue (Chad's is darker, indigo).
history: Adopted on 6 November 1959, ahead of independence from France on 11 August 1960. Originally designed with green at the hoist (echoing the green-yellow-red Pan-African scheme), but green was replaced by blue to avoid an exact match with the contemporary flag of Mali. The design closely resembles the flag of Romania, prompting Chad to raise the issue at the United Nations in 2004; no change has resulted.

features.symbolism: the design echoes the French tricolour but uses a blue–yellow–red palette combining the colours of Pan-Africanism and the French flag; blue for the sky and water, yellow for the sun and the Sahara, red for progress, unity and the blood shed for independence
